name: GT America Devanagari
classification_notes: GT America Devanagari extends the GT America grotesque sans-serif family into the Devanagari script, placing it squarely in the grotesque sans-serif tradition adapted for Indic typography.
summary: A Devanagari extension of the GT America grotesque family, bridging Latin and Indic script typesetting.
description: GT America Devanagari brings the GT America grotesque framework into the Devanagari script, allowing for consistent typographic voice across Latin and Indic settings. The typeface is offered through November, an Indian type foundry with a focus on multi-script typography. Beyond this, the page provides no detailed information about weights, widths or release year for this specific typeface.
foundry_blurb: November offers GT America Devanagari as part of their retail typeface catalogue, extending the well-known GT America grotesque into the Devanagari script for use across Latin and Indic typesetting contexts.

name: Delegate
classification_notes: Delegate sits squarely in the grotesque sans-serif tradition, explicitly positioned between cold European grotesks and warm American gothics, drawing on anonymous functional grotesque models.
summary: A grotesque sans exploring the middle ground between cold European grotesks and warm American gothics, published by Commercial Type in 2024.
description: Sitting between the anonymity of European functional grotesks and the warmth of American gothic tradition, Delegate aims for the kind of invisibility that comes from carefully considered idiosyncratic details rather than blandness. It was designed with functionalism as both purpose and aesthetic, making it suited to text-heavy applications where personality should remain understated. Published by Commercial Type in 2024, it is the studio type for November's own website.
foundry_blurb: November describe Delegate as exploring the grey area between cold European grotesks and warm American gothics, taking after anonymous, highly functional grotesques that remain invisible despite idiosyncratic shapes. They frame it as a hardworking sans that treats functionalism as both purpose and aesthetic, published by Commercial Type in 2024.

name: Graphik Devanagari
classification_notes: Graphik is a geometric grotesque sans-serif; the Devanagari extension follows the same rational, low-contrast approach adapted for the Devanagari script.
summary: A Devanagari extension of Commercial Type's Graphik, adapted for the script's formal requirements while preserving the original's rational, low-contrast character.
description: Graphik Devanagari brings the geometric grotesque aesthetic of Commercial Type's Graphik into the Devanagari script. The design maintains the restrained, functional tone of the Latin original, translating its neutral forms into a script with distinct structural demands. Suited to editorial, branding and interface work requiring consistent multi-script typesetting across Latin and Devanagari.
foundry_blurb: The page for Graphik Devanagari on the November foundry website did not load sufficient typeface-specific content to extract a foundry blurb; the page instead displayed unrelated editorial log entries and a notice about the site being typeset in Delegate.
